back: The first step of the lifecycle: bringing data into the lakehouse from apps, databases, files, and streams.
A Databricks tool that automatically and incrementally loads new files from cloud storage as they arrive.
Batch loads scheduled chunks of data; streaming flows in continuously as events happen. The lakehouse supports both.
You describe the refined result you want, and the platform builds, orders, runs, and quality-checks the transformation steps.
Automated, scheduled work that chains tasks (ingest → transform → train → report) with retries and alerts.
Dashboards and reports built on clean gold tables, helping the business see and act on its data.
A natural-language interface where business users ask questions in plain English and get charts, tables, and SQL back.
Training models (fraud, recommendations, forecasting) on the same governed data, tracked end to end by MLflow.
Chatbots and agents grounded in your own data using vector search and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
A managed database built into the platform that serves fresh data to live applications at low latency.
